title: Bryobium pudicum
taxonomy: Bryobium pudicum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The genus Bryobium belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae, which includes most of the modern orchids. Within the subfamily Epidendroideae, the genus Bryobium is part of the tribe Dendrobieae. The tribe Dendrobieae is characterized by epiphytic orchids with resupinate flowers.

intro: Bryobium pudicum, also known as the delicate orchid, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to tropical regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in wound healing, skin protection, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Bryobium pudicum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Bryobium pudicum is often used to prepare herbal infusions, extracts, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
